title: Diagnose 'fails in suite, passes alone' for a GenServer
prompt: A test `test/my_app/counter_test.exs` passes when run alone via `mix test test/my_app/counter_test.exs` but fails with `DBConnection.OwnershipError` when run as part of `mix test`. The module starts a GenServer at `setup_all` (not `setup`), and each test issues writes through the GenServer. Write `docs/diagnosis_fails_in_suite.md` explaining: (1) `setup_all` runs ONCE for the whole module, not per test, (2) the GenServer is owned by the setup_all process which has its own sandbox checkout, (3) each individual test has a DIFFERENT sandbox owner and the GenServer's connection is the setup_all's, (4) fix: move the GenServer start to `setup` (per-test).
tier_rationale: Requires connecting setup_all semantics with sandbox ownership. Two non-obvious concepts combined.
